book-align.title: Align book chapters 10/13/14 with autonomous-setup flow

installer-arm.title: Installer drops files only — stop auto-running add.py init so /add reaches the lock-down

question-summary-layer.title: The question is a summary, never the artifact

intake-interview.title: Interview before you size — proactive intent clarification at intake

ubiquitous-language.title: Replace method slang with domain-standard terminology

wave-ledger.title: WAVE.md — durable wave ledger for parallel streams

wave-status-hint.title: status surfaces a live WAVE.md as the wave resume hint

wave-protocol-runtime.title: streams.md: merge-time fork-base check + worker commits SUMMARY.md

engine-argv-portability.title: CLI accepts flags-before-slug on Python <=3.12 (argparse intermixing)

engine-merge-base-enforcement.title: Engine guards the merge-time fork-base echo on merge-back

history.reason: refute-read:adversarial refute-read: green not earned per frozen §3 — FG-1 any()-over-multi-sha-tokens lets a drift-note cell (mismatch + prefix-of-base token) pass BOTH surfaces; FG-2 unanchored re.search(status) lets body prose rescue a malformed header (check mis-classifies). Honest redo: all()-semantics on cell tokens + header-line-anchored status parse; suite untouched (coverage gap routes as delta).

refute-read:re-refute FG-3: (?m)^wave:.*?status: matches ANY wave:-prefixed line — a status-less header + a body line 'wave: ... status: live' rescues the parse; with a clean echo wave-verify exits 0 (true false-green). Honest redo: parse status from the FIRST wave: header line ONLY; suite untouched (vector routes as delta).

refute-read:3rd refute: P2b first |-row unconditionally consumed as column header — a headerless roster's mismatch row is SILENTLY skipped (both surfaces exit 0); P1 hardcoded cells[2] — an extra leading column hides the echo (check@live WARNs instead of FAILs); Pex (?m)^base:\s*(\S+) crosses the newline — an empty base: line parses as filled. Honest redo: first-base-line-only parse + header row MUST name a fork-base column (else malformed) + echo column derived from the header label.

refute-read:4th refute N1/N10: substring+first-wins echo-column pick ('fork-base' in c.lower(), next()) — a decoy header label 'fork-base-prev' steals the column; a FILLED MISMATCHED echo in the real fork-base column exits 0 on BOTH surfaces (contract: FILLED echo != base -> FAIL, any status). Heals 1-3 confirmed holding; clean template + short-sha confirmed. Fix shape: ambiguity refusal (>1 matching column -> wave_ledger_malformed) or exact label match.

refute-read:refute-read: 5th refute FG-STATUS-BOUNDARY: r'status:\s*(live|merging)\b' at the wave header parse — \b fires at non-word chars (| and -), so the prefix of an invalid status token parses as valid. Instance A (realistic): the EXACT unfilled streams.md template placeholder 'status: live|merging' + matching echo exits 0 on BOTH surfaces; A-harm: same placeholder + PENDING row leaves the standing monitor green (WARN only) — the merge-time fail-open this task exists to close. Contract: no status live|merging -> wave_ledger_malformed. Heals 1-3 + v2 ambiguity refusal CONFIRMED holding (7 known vectors re-executed, 24 new probes, this is the only failing family). Fix shape: strict status terminator (whitespace/separator/end-of-line only).

scope-decl-template.title: §5 BUILD declares its blast radius — Scope allowlist + Strategy batches, frozen with the bundle

scope-gate-enforce.title: the verify gate refuses a build that touched outside its declared §5 Scope

scope-violation-heal.title: a scope violation enters the bounded self-heal loop — honest redo, never a silent pass

next-footer-engine.title: mutating verbs print an engine-sourced next: footer — one resolver, no double-printing

gate-owner-marker.title: footer + guide name the driver — [you drive] vs [human gate] from autonomy × phase

ux-stale-followups.title: fold the v20 leftovers — soften the fresh-init placeholder goal + retire the stale milestone-done note

gitignore-scaffold.title: Scaffold .add/.gitignore at init to keep transient engine artifacts out of git

pty-clack-harness.title: Reusable PTY test helper for clack interactive paths

soul-seed-on-install.title: Seed .add/SOUL.md from bundled template during install and update

state-schema-migration.title: Multi-active state.json schema + idempotent fail-soft migration

active-accessors.title: Active milestone/task accessor seam; route all call sites

parallel-status-view.title: status renders the active set as parallel streams

engine-repin-parity.title: Consolidated 3-tree parity audit + ENGINE_MD5 + parity-test hardening

v1-1.title: v1.1 — adoption & ergonomics
v1-1.goal: make ADD easier to adopt and operate

v1-2.title: V1 2
v1-2.goal: Cross-milestone foundation (PROJECT.md) + dynamic CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md guideline injection

v2.title: Transparent · Provable · Minimal
v2.goal: Make ADD provable and explicitly two-surface (State/Story) without adding context-rot

v3.title: V3
v3.goal: Make ADD correct under misuse and safe to publish: refuse unsafe gates, guard the docs/build invariants, ship clean

v4-1.title: V4 1
v4-1.goal: Make ADD harness-drivable and self-scoping: machine-readable state (--json + owner/stop) plus an AI-facilitated request->versioned-milestone intake loop, framed as ADD

v6.title: The Self-Driving Run
v6.goal: Once a task's contract is frozen, run the build->verify half as a dynamic, self-improving workflow that converges in-run and auto-gates on evidence — pulling the human in only for the residue tests cannot catch — without losing ADD's human-led front or its delta learning.

v7.title: Auto by default — the one-approval flow
v7.goal: Make auto the default autonomy and compress the human-led front to a single approval at the contract-freeze seam, keeping the seam human and security as a HARD-STOP.

v8.title: AI-first install & milestone on-ramp
v8.goal: After install, a user develops with ADD by talking to the agent — request to milestone to one-approval run — with no add.py typing in the happy path; install output and onboarding teach that flow

v8-1.title: Guard the AI-first flow (runtime)
v8-1.goal: A project nudges work back toward the intake to milestone structure: add.py warns (never blocks) when a task lives outside a milestone, giving the documented AI-first flow a runtime backstop

v9.title: Awareness surface — render what happened
v9.goal: A person can see what just happened, what it cost, and what was learned — per-task phase results rolled up under a milestone retrospective — rendered on demand to stdout and persisted as RETRO.md at milestone close, without reading prose by hand

v9-1.title: Phase-detail drill-down
v9-1.goal: A person can drill into one task and read each phase's actual result — rules, scenarios, frozen contract, test plan, gate + evidence, observe delta — rendered read-only from its TASK.md, without opening the file

v10.title: Dogfood parallel-streams on fold-support tooling
v10.goal: Validate the new streams orchestration by shipping two independent fold-support features through it

v12.title: Autonomous Onboarding — zero-touch setup → human lock-down
v12.goal: Point ADD at any repo and it autonomously drafts the foundation, first-milestone scope, and a candidate first contract — silent for an existing codebase, interview-then-autonomous for a new one — all frozen at a single human lock-down.

build-scope-lock.title: Build Scope Lock
build-scope-lock.goal: a build's blast radius is declared before it starts and engine-enforced when it ends — touched is a subset of declared, so a passing build cannot quietly modify files outside its frozen scope

next-step-seams.title: Next Step Seams
next-step-seams.goal: after every mutating engine verb both the human and the AI see the engine-sourced next step and who drives it — next: footer plus [you drive] / [human gate] marker

delta-resolution.title: Delta Resolution
delta-resolution.goal: every delta — spec and competency — has a recorded, engine-driven resolution event, never a silent dangling lesson

delta-resolution-polish.title: Delta Resolution Polish
delta-resolution-polish.goal: polish the delta-resolution machinery from the deltas the first milestone surfaced: a true multi-file commit primitive (all-or-nothing across N files), a --match selector to target a specific open SPEC delta, and a compact --force override for an unrelated open SPEC delta

installer-smarts.title: Smart onboarding installer
installer-smarts.goal: On first run in a real terminal, add init onboards the user (brand, feature showcase, readiness, global-first scope, optional intent handoff to /add) instead of a silent file-drop; non-interactive/CI stays byte-identical.

scope-drafting-quality.title: Complete, well-positioned scope drafting
scope-drafting-quality.goal: Scope-drafting yields a complete, correctly-positioned MILESTONE.md — the guide covers the whole template shape (with a draft well-formedness gate) and requires grounding the goal in current assets + the milestone map before the goal sentence is drafted.

verify-expectations.title: Build-expectations in VERIFY
verify-expectations.goal: The §6 VERIFY step lets the AI pre-declare observable build expectations (derived from §2 scenarios + §3 contract) that verify confirms — so a build is checked correct, not merely test-green.

installer-smarts-polish.title: Installer Smarts Polish
installer-smarts-polish.goal: Close the PTY-only-reachable interactive-coverage gaps the installer-smarts gates disclosed: a reusable PTY test helper that drives clack select/confirm so the agent-select step and the clack happy-path prompts are exercised in CI (today they are node-syntax-checked + logic-unit-tested only).

installer-soul-seed.title: Installer seeds SOUL.md on install and update
installer-soul-seed.goal: When ADD is installed or updated, .add/SOUL.md is seeded from the bundled template if it does not yet exist — so the voice file is present from the first session without waiting for add.py init.

state-model-reshape.title: Multi-active state model + migration (team-collaboration foundation)
state-model-reshape.goal: ADD's engine tracks N truly-parallel active milestones — state.json holds a SET of active milestones, each with its own active task — with a backward-compatible migration from the single-active schema and the ENGINE_MD5 pin deliberately re-established.

archived.title: Decision-seam reports — decisive facts first, engine-sourced
Ground phase — build against the real codebase
Verify integrity — prove the green was earned, not gamed
Foundation compaction — every survivor spec shrinks too
Decision suggestions — a recommended pick + described alternatives at every human gate
